last_item: Dashboard — make the 'no backup yet' callout's 'Backups page' an actual <Link to="/backups"> (med/S).
last_result: REJECTED on restraint (full review panel). Implemented the link (matching Backups.tsx's existing inline <Link>), wrapped test renders in MemoryRouter, asserted href; test passed. Panel: 3 SHIP (UX heuristics — recognition-over-recall + consistency; Sam — one-click trail that doesn't dead-end; Priya — gets out of the way, no new control) but the RESTRAINT CRITIC BLOCKED: decorative payoff — the copy already names the destination and the persistent left-nav is one obvious click from every view, so the link only shaves a click off an empty state most users see ~once; no task was blocked or harder before. Per the contract the restraint blocker is never overruled by the others, so the change does NOT ship. Reverted both files clean; recorded the lesson ('matches an existing pattern' justifies a link you're adding for a real need — it doesn't manufacture the need; a same-name link to a top-level nav destination is the weak case) in learnings.md; marked backlog item rejected. No code shipped. Next top item: Query — surface the server's executed_sql (possibly LIMIT-rewritten) in a compact collapsed block so users see what actually ran (med/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
